perturbation.original_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party shall promptly return or destroy all copies (in whatever form reproduced or stored), including all notes and derivatives of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 The Receiving Party should endeavor to return or destroy copies of the Confidential Information disclosed under this Agreement, as reasonably practical, upon the earlier of (i) the completion or termination of the dealings contemplated in this Agreement; (ii) or the termination of this Agreement; (iii) or at the time the Disclosing Party may request it to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text creates a clear obligation to return or destroy all copies. Changing 'shall promptly return or destroy' to 'should endeavor to return or destroy, as reasonably practical' introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation, making it subject to interpretation and potentially allowing the Receiving Party to avoid compliance under certain circumstances.
perturbation.contradicted_law: German Civil Code (Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch – BGB) § 241 Obligation to perform
perturbation.law_explanation: German law, specifically BGB § 241, establishes the fundamental principle that contractual obligations must be performed as agreed upon. By weakening the obligation from 'shall promptly' to 'should endeavor, as reasonably practical,' the modification contradicts the principle of faithful contract performance required under German law. 'Reasonably practical' introduces subjectivity, potentially allowing the Receiving Party to avoid the clear obligation to return or destroy confidential information, undermining the contract's intent.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 241 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 241 Pflichten aus dem Schuldverhältnis (1) Kraft des Schuldverhältnisses ist der Gläubiger berechtigt, von dem Schuldner eine Leistung zu fordern. Die Leistung kann auch in einem Unterlassen bestehen. (2) Das Schuldverhältnis kann nach seinem Inhalt jeden Teil zur Rücksicht auf die Rechte, Rechtsgüter und Interessen des anderen Teils verpflichten. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The original contract language mandates a specific action ('shall promptly return or destroy'), while the changed language weakens this obligation ('should endeavor to return or destroy, as reasonably practical'). This introduces ambiguity and subjectivity, potentially allowing the Receiving Party to avoid compliance. BGB § 241, as shown in both scraped snippets, establishes the principle that contractual obligations must be performed ('Kraft des Schuldverhältnisses ist der Gläubiger berechtigt, von dem Schuldner eine Leistung zu fordern.'). By making the obligation less definitive, the modified text contradicts this principle.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its employees, representatives or agents who need to have access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of carrying out their duties in connection with the permitted purposes specified in clause 2. The Receiving Party will inform them about the confidential quality of the information provided and will ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement. Hence, the Receiving Party w ill be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 The Receiving Party will not disclose and will keep confidential the information received, except to its employees, representatives or agents who may need to have access to the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party will attempt to inform them about the confidential quality of the information provided and seek their agreement to keep it confidential. Hence, the Receiving Party will be responsible for the actions of its personnel.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause imposes a strict obligation on the Receiving Party to ensure confidentiality among its employees, representatives and agents. The modified text weakens this obligation by changing 'who need to have access' to 'who may need to have access,' making it more subjective and less defined. Furthermore, replacing 'will inform them about the confidential quality' with 'will attempt to inform them' and removing 'ensure that their agreement is obtained to keep it confidential on the same terms as set forth in this Agreement' introduces ambiguity, creating a weaker standard of compliance and potentially allowing the Receiving Party to avoid liability. By only stating 'responsible for the actions of its personnel' removes the 'full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.'
perturbation.contradicted_law: German Civil Code (Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch – BGB) § 278 Responsibility of the obligor for third parties
perturbation.law_explanation: The original clause intends to comply with German Civil Code (BGB) § 278, which holds a party responsible for the actions of third parties (e.g., employees, agents) they use to fulfill their contractual obligations. By changing the wording, the modification creates a loophole that reduces the Receiving Party's liability for breaches of confidentiality by its personnel, thus potentially contradicting the level of responsibility required under German law. The original contract was clear with ensuring that the receiving party 'will be responsible for ensuring that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents.'
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 278 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 278 Verantwortlichkeit des Schuldners für Dritte Der Schuldner hat ein Verschulden seines gesetzlichen Vertreters und der Personen, deren er sich zur Erfüllung seiner Verbindlichkeit bedient, in gleichem Umfang zu vertreten wie eigenes Verschulden. Die Vorschrift des § 276 Abs. 3 findet keine Anwendung. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the Receiving Party's obligation regarding confidentiality, potentially contradicting German Civil Code (BGB) § 278, which holds a party responsible for the actions of third parties used to fulfill contractual obligations. The original text uses 'will ensure that the obligations of confidentiality and non -use contained herein will be strictly observed and will assume full liability for the acts or omissions made for its personnel representatives or agents', which creates a strict obligation for the receiving party, whereas the modified text uses 'will attempt to inform them about the confidential quality' and 'responsible for the actions of its personnel,' which creates ambiguity by weakening the standard of compliance and potentially reducing the Receiving Party's liability. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states: '§ 278 Verantwortlichkeit des Schuldners für Dritte Der Schuldner hat ein Verschulden seines gesetzlichen Vertreters und der Personen, deren er sich zur Erfüllung seiner Verbindlichkeit bedient, in gleichem Umfang zu vertreten wie eigenes Verschulden.', which supports the argument that the modified contract is in contradiction with the law.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1.1 This Agreement will enter into force on the date on which it is signed. It will remain in force until the ILDA -care project is finalised . Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that wa s disclosed during the term shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1.1 This Agreement will enter into force on the date on which it is signed. It will remain in force until the ILDA -care project is finalised . Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party’s duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information shall remain in effect unless deemed unnecessary.
perturbation.explanation: The original text establishes an indefinite duty to maintain confidentiality, which provides strong protection for the Disclosing Party. The modified text replaces 'shall remain in effect indefinitely, save otherwise agreed' with 'shall remain in effect unless deemed unnecessary'. This introduces a significant ambiguity and loophole, potentially allowing the Receiving Party to unilaterally determine that the confidentiality duty is no longer required and terminate the obligation. This is a contradiction to the expected legal framework, which would expect a termination only when agreed upon by all parties.
perturbation.contradicted_law: German Civil Code (Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch – BGB) § 311 Principles of performance and interpretation
perturbation.law_explanation: The original clause intends to comply with German Civil Code (BGB) § 311, the principles of interpretation of contracts under German law, by clearly defining the duration of the confidentiality obligation. The modification, by introducing the term 'unless deemed unnecessary', creates an undefined and subjective condition for termination, undermining the principle of good faith and fair dealing in contract interpretation. It permits the Receiving Party to unilaterally alter a fundamental term of the agreement based on their own subjective assessment, contradicting the standard for reasonable and fair interpretation required by German law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 311 BGB - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch (BGB) § 311 Rechtsgeschäftliche und rechtsgeschäftsähnliche Schuldverhältnisse (1) Zur Begründung eines Schuldverhältnisses durch Rechtsgeschäft sowie zur Änderung des Inhalts eines Schuldverhältnisses ist ein Vertrag zwischen den Beteiligten erforderlich, soweit nicht das Gesetz ein anderes vorschreibt. (2) Ein Schuldverhältnis mit Pflichten nach § 241 Abs. 2 entsteht auch durch 1. die Aufnahme von Vertragsverhandlungen, 2. die Anbahnung eines Vertrags, bei welcher der eine Teil im Hinblick auf eine etwaige rechtsgeschäftliche Beziehung dem anderen Teil die Möglichkeit zur Einwirkung auf seine Rechte, Rechtsgüter und Interessen gewährt oder ihm diese anvertraut, oder 3. ähnliche geschäftliche Kontakte. (3) Ein Schuldverhältnis mit Pflichten nach § 241 Abs. 2 kann auch zu Personen entstehen, die nicht selbst Vertragspartei werden sollen. Ein solches Schuldverhältnis entsteht insbesondere, wenn der Dritte in besonderem Maße Vertrauen für sich in Anspruch nimmt und dadurch die Vertragsverhandlungen oder den Vertragsschluss erheblich beeinflusst. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a significant ambiguity regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligation, potentially allowing the Receiving Party to unilaterally terminate it. This directly contradicts the principle outlined in § 311 BGB, which requires a contract between the involved parties for any alteration of the terms of their agreement. The original contract text established an indefinite duty, whereas the altered text allows the receiving party to deem the obligation unnecessary. This violates the requirement for mutual agreement in contractual changes, as stated in Snippet 1: 'Zur Begründung eines Schuldverhältnisses durch Rechtsgeschäft sowie zur Änderung des Inhalts eines Schuldverhältnisses ist ein Vertrag zwischen den Beteiligten erforderlich, soweit nicht das Gesetz ein anderes vorschreibt.'

perturbation.original_text: 3.9 In the event that the Receiving Party is asked to communicate the Confidential Information to any judicial, administrative, regulatory authority or similar or oblige d to reveal such information by mandatory law, it shall notify promptly the Disclosing Party of the terms of such disclosure and will collaborate to the extent practicable with the Disclosing Party in order to comply with the order and preserve the confide ntiality of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.9 In the event that the Receiving Party is asked to communicate the Confidential Information to any judicial, administrative, regulatory authority or similar, or obliged to reveal such information by mandatory law, it may notify the Disclosing Party and consider collaboration to comply with the order.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause creates a clear obligation for the Receiving Party to notify the Disclosing Party promptly and collaborate to comply with the order and preserve confidentiality. The modified text changes 'it shall notify promptly' to 'it may notify' and 'will collaborate to the extent practicable' to 'consider collaboration.' This introduces significant ambiguity and weakens the obligation, making notification and collaboration discretionary rather than mandatory. This weakening could lead to non-compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_law: German Civil Code (Bürgerliches Gesetzbuch – BGB) § 242 Performance in good faith
perturbation.law_explanation: The original clause intends to ensure compliance with the principle of good faith, as enshrined in BGB § 242, requiring parties to act honestly and fairly in the performance of their contractual obligations. The modification introduces ambiguity by making notification and collaboration optional, which may violate the good faith principle. By not requiring prompt notification and genuine collaboration, the Receiving Party can potentially act in its own self-interest, failing to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information as reasonably expected under German law. This can create a conflict of interest.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the Receiving Party's obligation to notify and collaborate with the Disclosing Party, changing 'shall notify promptly' to 'may notify' and 'will collaborate' to 'consider collaboration.' This introduces ambiguity and makes these actions discretionary, undermining the principle of good faith. The Law Explanation states this could allow the Receiving Party to act in its self-interest, failing to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information. Snippet 2 states, 'Der Schuldner ist verpflichtet, die Leistung so zu bewirken, wie Treu und Glauben mit Rücksicht auf die Verkehrssitte es erfordern,' which translates to 'The debtor is obliged to effect the performance in such a way as good faith with regard to commercial practice requires.' By making notification and collaboration optional, the modified clause potentially violates this obligation of good faith, creating a contradiction.
